commit.message:
fix: remove lexical from peerDependencies — bundled as dependency

lexical is an internal implementation detail. Users never touch the
Lexical API directly (no public types, no exported Lexical instances).
Making it a peer was wrong — it required users to install an extra
package they never use.

- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/core
- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:
  pnpm add @lexify/core

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: CI testing, dev/ structure, TimelineLayer, and coverage reporting (#544)

* fix(website): remove three/wild-forest from sidebar until doc page exists

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(ci): add PR test workflow, vitest coverage, and unit tests

Closes a degree of #82 by establishing an automated test baseline for every PR.
Applies reviewer feedback from #512 re: dev/ directory for WIP/RFC content.

## CI workflow (.github/workflows/test.yml)
- Triggers on push to master and all pull_request events
- lint job: runs yarn lint (no build required)
- test-node job: builds, runs vitest node project with v8 coverage, uploads to Coveralls
- test-headless job: installs Playwright Chromium, runs headless browser tests

Modeled after deck.gl's test.yml (see visgl/deck.gl vitest-migration-rfc) using
@vitest/browser + Playwright as first-class visgl testing utilities.

## Coverage (vitest.config.ts + package.json)
- Added test.coverage block: provider v8, reporters text + lcov (for Coveralls)
- Scoped to modules/*/src/**, excludes basemap-props* and template
- Added test-ci script: vitest run --project node --coverage
- Added @vitest/coverage-v8 devDependency

## New unit tests (all 308 pass, 60 test files)
- modules/editable-layers/test/immutable-feature-collection.node.spec.ts
  ImmutableFeatureCollection: add/delete/replace/removePosition, polygon ring
  wrapping, 3D elevation preservation, immutability guarantees (17 tests)
- modules/editable-layers/test/imports.node.spec.ts
  Public API surface smoke test: draw, alter, and measurement mode exports (6 tests)
- modules/layers/test/imports.node.spec.ts (2 tests)
- modules/widgets/test/imports.node.spec.ts (4 tests)
- modules/geo-layers/test/imports.node.spec.ts (3 tests)

## dev/ directory
Per ibgreen's feedback in #512: establishes dev/ as the canonical home for RFCs
and WIP content that should NOT be indexed by the docs site search.

commit.message:
ci: add path filters to non-core workflows (#3306)

## What changed
Added path filters to 3 workflows that were triggering on every push/PR
regardless of files changed:

- **guards.yml**: Now filters to \.github/**\, \scripts/**\,
\crates/**\, \Cargo.*\, \ ust-toolchain.toml\
- **feature-matrix.yml**: Now filters to \crates/**\, \Cargo.*\, \
ust-toolchain.toml\
- **property-tests.yml**: Now filters to \crates/**\, \Cargo.*\, \
ust-toolchain.toml\, \ uzz/**\

Schedule and workflow_dispatch triggers are preserved.

## Why
These workflows were consuming GitHub Actions runner capacity on every
push (including docs-only changes), blocking CI Core (the merge gate)
from getting runners promptly. We observed 60+ non-core workflows queued
competing with CI Core for runners.

## What was tested
- YAML syntax is valid (follows existing ci-core.yml pattern)
- CI will validate the workflows parse correctly
- No behavioral change for Rust source changes (same triggers, just
filtered)

## Risks
- If we add a new top-level directory with Rust code, we'd need to add
it to the path filters. Low risk since the repo structure is stable.

commit.message:
perf(inference): eliminate per-token/per-head allocations in dense forward (#3305)

## What changed
- Added \ms_norm_into()\ that writes to a pre-allocated output buffer
- \DenseTransformerBlock::forward()\: single \
ormed\ buffer allocated once and reused for both attention-norm and
FFN-norm passes (previously 2seq_len separate Vec allocations per block)
- \DenseModel::forward()\: uses \ms_norm_into()\ for final norm
- \DenseAttention::forward()\: hoists \scores\ and \weights\ vectors out
of the inner (head position) loop into a single pair of pre-allocated
buffers (previously O(num_heads seq_len) allocations)

## Why
The dense forward pass allocated temporary vectors inside tight loops:
- RMSNorm: \ ms_norm()\ returned a new \Vec<f32>\ per token per layer,
then \xtend()\d into the normed buffer O(layers seq_len) allocations
- Attention: \scores\ and \weights\ vectors allocated per head per
position O(num_heads seq_len) allocations

For a 32-head, 24-layer model with 512-token context: ~24K norm allocs +
~8M attention allocs eliminated.

## What was tested
- All 19 dense_forward tests pass (including new \
est_rms_norm_into_matches_rms_norm\)
- \cargo test -p bitnet-inference --lib dense_forward
--no-default-features --features cpu\
- No behavioral change (pure refactor: same outputs, fewer allocations)

## Risks
None \ ms_norm()\ now delegates to \ ms_norm_into()\, so existing
callers are unaffected.

## Triage: A (MERGE NOW)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: exponential backoff between BT reconnect attempts

Without backoff, a stuck device (e.g. powered off) fires bluetoothctl
connect every ~21s indefinitely, causing ALSA underruns on other A2DP
devices sharing the same adapter due to BT radio contention.

_reconnect_delay(attempt):
  - attempts 1-3 : check_interval (10s, unchanged)
  - attempt 4    : 20s
  - attempt 5    : 40s
  - attempt 6    : 80s
  - attempt 7+   : 160s → capped at 300s (5 min)

Applied to both _monitor_dbus (explicit sleep) and _monitor_polling
(advances last_check to defer the next poll cycle).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove never-hit e2e caches (~2.5 min/run saved) (#390)

* Initial plan

* Remove unused e2e caches that are never hit (~2.5 min/run saved)

Investigation of recent CI runs confirmed all three caches (Docker
images, node_modules, Playwright browsers) always showed "Cache not
found" — because e2e only runs on PRs (never main), so branch-scoped
GitHub Actions caches are never populated for PR branches to inherit.

Removes:
- 3 actions/cache steps (Docker images, node_modules, Playwright)
- Explicit "Save Docker images for caching" step
- Conditional if guards on Docker Buildx/login/build steps
- Cache-hit conditional logic in the install step

Keeps:
- Docker BuildKit layer caching (type=gha) which works cross-branch

commit.message:
fix: coerce numeric appId/appSecret to string when reading config (#9)

When using `openclaw config set 'channels.weibo.appId' '123456789'`,
YAML/JSON may store the value as a number. The previous
readOptionalNonBlankString helper returned undefined for non-string
values, causing the account to appear as unconfigured.

Fix: coerce finite numbers to strings, matching the existing coercion
already present in token.ts (String(appId)).
